notes: EDA corrects spec on several key points: (1) Silver onet_occupations = 798 rows, not ~867 (69 BLS SOCs truly empty at BLS level, not 93). (2) 24 partial-data occupations, not 29. (3) 4 of 9 burnout element IDs were wrong in spec — corrected IDs from EDA used here. (4) Career transitions = 15,944 after dedup/self-ref removal. All thresholds evidence-based from EDA.

rules.name: onet_occupations: bls_soc_code format XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_occupations WHERE bls_soc_code IS NULL OR bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every bls_soc_code must match the BLS SOC standard format XX-XXXX (2-digit major group, hyphen, 4-digit detail). This is the primary cross-source join key for BLS OOH and CIP-SOC crosswalk.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 867 derivable BLS SOC codes from Bronze match ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ with zero violations. Domain context confirms SOC 2018 XX-XXXX as the authoritative standard. 100% threshold is achievable.

rules.name: onet_occupations: bls_soc_code uniqueness (grain integrity)
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_occupations GROUP BY bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per bls_soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a transformer or promote failure. This is the structural foundation of the occupation dimension table.
rules.rationale: EDA: 867 unique BLS SOC codes derivable by truncation from 1,016 O*NET-SOC codes. After excluding 69 truly empty BLS SOCs, 798 unique rows expected. Zero duplicates observed. Logical model declares bls_soc_code as the natural key.

rules.name: onet_occupations: row count = 798
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 798 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_occupations
rules.description: Silver onet_occupations must contain exactly 798 rows: 867 derivable BLS SOCs minus 69 truly empty BLS SOCs (those with zero child data at BLS granularity).
rules.rationale: EDA: 867 unique BLS SOC codes, 69 truly empty at BLS level. 798 = 867 - 69. The spec estimated ~867 but EDA proves 24 of the 93 zero-data O*NET codes share a BLS prefix with codes that DO have data, so only 69 BLS SOCs are excluded.

rules.name: onet_occupations: multi_detail_flag count = 76
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 76 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_occupations WHERE multi_detail_flag = true
rules.description: Exactly 76 BLS SOCs should have multi_detail_flag = true (multiple O*NET detail codes mapping to one BLS SOC).
rules.rationale: EDA: Confirmed 76 BLS SOCs with multiple O*NET detail codes (range 2-10 details). 791 have exactly 1 detail code. This count is deterministic from the source data.

rules.name: onet_occupations: data_completeness_tier distribution
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN full_count = 774 AND partial_count = 24 AND none_count = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN data_completeness_tier = 'full' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS full_count, SUM(CASE WHEN data_completeness_tier = 'partial' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS partial_count, SUM(CASE WHEN data_completeness_tier NOT IN ('full', 'partial') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS none_count FROM base.onet_occupations)
rules.description: Expect exactly 774 'full' BLS SOCs, 24 'partial' (tasks + related only), and zero 'none' (excluded from Silver). Only valid values are 'full' and 'partial'.
rules.rationale: EDA: At BLS level, 774 full (all 4 child tables), 24 partial (tasks + related only), 69 none (excluded from Silver). Logical model confirms valid values are {full, partial} only.

rules.name: onet_occupations: no 'All Other' or Military occupations
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_occupations WHERE primary_title LIKE '%All Other%' AND data_completeness_tier = 'none'
rules.description: No BLS SOCs with data_completeness_tier = 'none' should be present in Silver. The 93 'All Other'/Military O*NET codes (69 at BLS level) with zero child data must be excluded.
rules.rationale: EDA: 93 O*NET-SOC codes with zero data in all child tables. At BLS level, 69 are truly empty (24 share a prefix with data-bearing detail codes). None-tier occupations must be filtered out during Silver transformation.

rules.name: onet_occupations: all required fields NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_occupations WHERE record_id IS NULL OR bls_soc_code IS NULL OR primary_title IS NULL OR description IS NULL OR onet_detail_codes IS NULL OR onet_detail_count IS NULL OR multi_detail_flag IS NULL OR has_work_activities IS NULL OR has_work_context IS NULL OR has_tasks IS NULL OR has_related IS NULL OR data_completeness_tier IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 14 columns in onet_occupations are defined as NOT NULL in the logical model. Any null indicates a transformer bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model: All 14 attributes are NOT NULL. EDA confirms source data is complete for all retained occupations. If a row exists in Silver, all fields must be populated.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: grain uniqueness (bls_soc_code x element_id)
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, element_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_activity_profiles GROUP BY bls_soc_code, element_id HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is bls_soc_code x element_id. Any duplicate indicates a multi-detail aggregation or promote failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 894 O*NET SOCs x 41 elements = 36,654 IM rows in Bronze. After BLS aggregation to 774 SOCs x 41 = 31,734 rows. Logical model declares composite natural key. Zero duplicates expected.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: importance range 1.0-5.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_activity_profiles WHERE importance < 1.0 OR importance > 5.0 OR importance IS NULL
rules.description: IM scale importance values must fall within the O*NET defined range of 1.0 to 5.0. Values outside this range indicate a transformation or averaging error.
rules.rationale: EDA: IM scale observed range is 1.00-4.99 across 36,654 Bronze rows. The scale maximum is 5.0 (just not observed in current data). Multi-detail averaging cannot produce values outside [1.0, 5.0] since it averages values within that range.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: exactly 41 distinct element_ids
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT element_id) = 41 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_activity_profiles
rules.description: There must be exactly 41 distinct Work Activity element IDs (Generalized Work Activities from the O*NET Content Model).
rules.rationale: EDA: Exactly 41 distinct IM elements confirmed across all 894 O*NET SOCs. Every occupation has all 41 activities. This count is structural to the O*NET Content Model.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: importance_rank 1-41 per occupation, no gaps
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code FROM base.onet_activity_profiles GROUP BY bls_soc_code HAVING MIN(importance_rank) != 1 OR MAX(importance_rank) != 41 OR COUNT(DISTINCT importance_rank) != 41
rules.description: Each occupation must have importance_rank values forming a complete 1-41 sequence with no gaps or duplicates. Rank 1 = most important activity, 41 = least.
rules.rationale: EDA: Every occupation has exactly 41 IM elements. Ranks are derived by ordering importance descending within each bls_soc_code. With 41 elements per occupation, ranks must span 1-41 without gaps.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: suppress_flag < 1%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN suppress_flag = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) < 1.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_activity_profiles
rules.description: Less than 1% of activity profile rows should have suppress_flag = true. Higher rates indicate unexpected data quality issues in the Bronze source.
rules.rationale: EDA: Only 1 of 36,654 IM rows (0.003%) has recommend_suppress = 'Y'. After BLS aggregation, at most 1 BLS SOC x element pair has suppress_flag = true. Threshold of <1% provides generous headroom above the observed 0.003%.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: row count = 31,734
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 31734 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_activity_profiles
rules.description: Expected exactly 31,734 rows (774 BLS SOCs with WA data x 41 activities). Only occupations with Work Activities data contribute rows.
rules.rationale: EDA: 774 BLS SOCs have Work Activities data. Every occupation has all 41 IM elements. 774 x 41 = 31,734. The 24 partial-data occupations have no Work Activities.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: FK to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT a.bls_soc_code FROM base.onet_activity_profiles a LEFT JOIN base.onet_occupations o ON a.bls_soc_code = o.bls_soc_code WHERE o.bls_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every bls_soc_code in activity_profiles must exist in onet_occupations. Orphan activity profiles indicate a filtering or join error.
rules.rationale: EDA: All bls_soc_codes in activity_profiles (774 BLS SOCs) are a subset of the 798 in onet_occupations. Logical model declares this as a foreign key relationship.

rules.name: onet_activity_profiles: all required fields NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_activity_profiles WHERE record_id IS NULL OR bls_soc_code IS NULL OR element_id IS NULL OR element_name IS NULL OR importance IS NULL OR importance_rank IS NULL OR is_high_importance IS NULL OR onet_details_averaged IS NULL OR suppress_flag IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 11 columns in onet_activity_profiles are defined as NOT NULL in the logical model.
rules.rationale: Logical model: All 11 attributes are NOT NULL. EDA confirms O*NET provides complete ratings for all retained occupation-element combinations. No nulls expected.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: grain uniqueness (bls_soc_code x element_id)
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, element_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_context_profiles GROUP BY bls_soc_code, element_id HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is bls_soc_code x element_id. Any duplicate indicates a scale filtering or aggregation failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 894 O*NET SOCs x 57 CX/CT elements = 50,958 rows in Bronze. After BLS aggregation to 774 SOCs x 57 = 44,118 rows. Logical model declares composite natural key. Each element_id maps to exactly one scale (CX or CT), so no scale-based duplication possible.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: CX context_value range 1.0-5.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_context_profiles WHERE scale_id = 'CX' AND (context_value < 1.0 OR context_value > 5.0 OR context_value IS NULL)
rules.description: CX scale context values must fall within O*NET defined range of 1.0 to 5.0.
rules.rationale: EDA: CX observed range is 1.00-5.00 across 49,170 Bronze rows (55 elements). Multi-detail averaging preserves the range. Scale maximum is fully utilized.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: CT context_value range 1.0-3.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_context_profiles WHERE scale_id = 'CT' AND (context_value < 1.0 OR context_value > 3.0 OR context_value IS NULL)
rules.description: CT scale context values must fall within O*NET defined range of 1.0 to 3.0. CT covers 2 elements: Duration of Typical Work Week and Work Schedules.
rules.rationale: EDA: CT observed range is 1.00-3.00 across 1,788 Bronze rows (2 elements). Scale boundaries are fully utilized.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: exactly 57 distinct element_ids
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT element_id) = 57 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_context_profiles
rules.description: There must be exactly 57 distinct Work Context element IDs (55 CX + 2 CT from the O*NET Content Model).
rules.rationale: EDA: Exactly 57 CX/CT elements confirmed across all 894 O*NET SOCs. 55 elements on CX scale, 2 on CT scale. This count is structural to the O*NET Content Model.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: is_burnout_element correct count (9 element_ids)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT element_id) = 9 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_context_profiles WHERE is_burnout_element = true
rules.description: Exactly 9 distinct element_ids should have is_burnout_element = true, using the EDA-corrected IDs: 4.C.3.d.1, 4.C.3.d.8, 4.C.3.a.1, 4.C.3.d.3, 4.C.3.a.2.b, 4.C.3.b.4, 4.C.3.b.7, 4.C.3.d.4, 4.C.3.a.2.a.
rules.rationale: EDA CRITICAL: 4 of 9 spec-proposed burnout IDs were wrong. Corrected list validated against actual Bronze element IDs. 9 elements confirmed present in the data. See EDA 'Corrected Burnout Element Mapping' section.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: no CXP/CTP scale rows
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_context_profiles WHERE scale_id NOT IN ('CX', 'CT')
rules.description: Silver context_profiles must contain only CX and CT scale rows. CXP/CTP category-percentage rows (82.9% of Bronze Work Context) must be excluded.
rules.rationale: EDA: CX/CT represent 17.1% of Bronze Work Context rows (50,958 of 297,676). Spec confirms CXP/CTP retained in Bronze only. Any non-CX/CT row in Silver indicates a filter failure.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: FK to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.bls_soc_code FROM base.onet_context_profiles c LEFT JOIN base.onet_occupations o ON c.bls_soc_code = o.bls_soc_code WHERE o.bls_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every bls_soc_code in context_profiles must exist in onet_occupations. Orphan context profiles indicate a filtering or join error.
rules.rationale: EDA: All bls_soc_codes in context_profiles (774 BLS SOCs) are a subset of the 798 in onet_occupations. Logical model declares this as a foreign key relationship.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: row count = 44,118
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 44118 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_context_profiles
rules.description: Expected exactly 44,118 rows (774 BLS SOCs with WC data x 57 elements). Only occupations with Work Context data contribute rows.
rules.rationale: EDA: 774 BLS SOCs have Work Context data (perfectly synchronized with Work Activities). Every occupation has all 57 CX/CT elements. 774 x 57 = 44,118.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: all required fields NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_context_profiles WHERE record_id IS NULL OR bls_soc_code IS NULL OR element_id IS NULL OR element_name IS NULL OR scale_id IS NULL OR context_value IS NULL OR is_burnout_element IS NULL OR onet_details_averaged IS NULL OR suppress_flag IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 11 columns in onet_context_profiles are defined as NOT NULL in the logical model.
rules.rationale: Logical model: All 11 attributes are NOT NULL. EDA confirms O*NET provides complete CX/CT ratings for all retained occupation-element combinations.

rules.name: onet_context_profiles: suppress_flag < 1%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN suppress_flag = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) < 1.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_context_profiles
rules.description: Less than 1% of context profile rows should have suppress_flag = true.
rules.rationale: EDA: 18 of 49,170 CX rows (0.04%) and 0 CT rows have recommend_suppress = 'Y'. After BLS aggregation, at most 18 pairs. Threshold of <1% provides generous headroom above the observed 0.04%.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: grain uniqueness (bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code)
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, related_bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_career_transitions GROUP BY bls_soc_code, related_bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code. Duplicates indicate deduplication failure during BLS-level aggregation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 2,173 duplicate BLS pairs exist in raw data from multi-detail O*NET pairings. After MIN(related_index) dedup, 15,944 unique pairs remain. Logical model declares composite natural key.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: no self-references
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE bls_soc_code = related_bls_soc_code
rules.description: No career transition should reference itself (bls_soc_code = related_bls_soc_code). Self-references emerge during BLS-level aggregation (343 raw) and must be excluded.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 self-references at O*NET level, but 343 emerge after BLS truncation (when two O*NET details of the same BLS SOC relate to each other). All must be filtered out. Spec explicitly requires exclusion.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: best_index range 1-20
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE best_index < 1 OR best_index > 20 OR best_index IS NULL
rules.description: best_index must be in range 1-20 (O*NET Related Occupations provides up to 20 related occupations per source). Lower = more similar.
rules.rationale: EDA: Related index range in Bronze is 1-20. MIN(related_index) aggregation preserves the lower bound. No values outside 1-20 observed.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: relatedness_tier exactly 3 values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE relatedness_tier NOT IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long', 'Supplemental')
rules.description: relatedness_tier must be exactly one of: 'Primary-Short' (index 1-5), 'Primary-Long' (index 6-10), 'Supplemental' (index 11-20).
rules.rationale: EDA: Tier distribution after dedup: Primary-Short 4,134 (25.9%), Primary-Long 3,938 (24.7%), Supplemental 7,872 (49.4%). All rows map to exactly one of 3 tiers. Derived deterministically from best_index.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: is_primary consistency with tier
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE (is_primary = true AND relatedness_tier = 'Supplemental') OR (is_primary = false AND relatedness_tier IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long'))
rules.description: is_primary must be true when relatedness_tier is 'Primary-Short' or 'Primary-Long', and false when 'Supplemental'. These are derived from the same source (best_index) so must be consistent.
rules.rationale: Logical model: is_primary = relatedness_tier IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long'). Both are derived from best_index. Any inconsistency is a derivation bug.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: FK bls_soc_code to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT t.bls_soc_code FROM base.onet_career_transitions t LEFT JOIN base.onet_occupations o ON t.bls_soc_code = o.bls_soc_code WHERE o.bls_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every source bls_soc_code in career_transitions must exist in onet_occupations.
rules.rationale: EDA: 798 distinct source BLS SOCs in career transitions. All are expected to be in the 798-row onet_occupations table. Logical model declares FK relationship.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: FK related_bls_soc_code to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT t.related_bls_soc_code FROM base.onet_career_transitions t LEFT JOIN base.onet_occupations o ON t.related_bls_soc_code = o.bls_soc_code WHERE o.bls_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every target related_bls_soc_code in career_transitions must exist in onet_occupations. Orphan targets indicate an exclusion filter missed the 'none'-tier SOCs.
rules.rationale: EDA: All target BLS SOCs in career transitions resolve to occupations with data. The 'none'-tier SOCs are already absent from Related Occupations in Bronze.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: row count = 15,944
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 15944 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_career_transitions
rules.description: Expected exactly 15,944 rows after BLS-level aggregation, self-reference removal, and deduplication.
rules.rationale: EDA: 18,460 raw -> 343 self-refs removed -> 18,117 -> 2,173 deduped -> 15,944 unique BLS pairs. No none-tier SOCs to exclude (already absent from Related Occupations).

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: both SOC codes valid XX-XXXX format
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$' OR related_bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Both bls_soc_code and related_bls_soc_code must match XX-XXXX format. Invalid formats indicate a truncation error during BLS-level aggregation.
rules.rationale: EDA: All O*NET-SOC codes follow XX-XXXX.XX format. Truncation to first 7 characters produces XX-XXXX. Zero format violations expected.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: all required fields NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE record_id IS NULL OR bls_soc_code IS NULL OR related_bls_soc_code IS NULL OR best_index IS NULL OR relatedness_tier IS NULL OR is_primary IS NULL OR relationship_type IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 9 columns in onet_career_transitions are defined as NOT NULL in the logical model.
rules.rationale: Logical model: All 9 attributes are NOT NULL. Related Occupations in Bronze has no nulls in key fields. Derived fields (tier, is_primary, relationship_type) are computed deterministically.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: relationship_type always 'similarity'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE relationship_type != 'similarity'
rules.description: relationship_type must always be 'similarity' for this source (Related Occupations). Future sources may add 'transition' type.
rules.rationale: Logical model: Static value 'similarity' for all rows. Career Changers/Starters matrices do not exist in O*NET 30.2. Any non-'similarity' value is a transformer bug.

rules.name: onet_career_transitions: tier-to-best_index consistency
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_career_transitions WHERE (relatedness_tier = 'Primary-Short' AND (best_index < 1 OR best_index > 5)) OR (relatedness_tier = 'Primary-Long' AND (best_index < 6 OR best_index > 10)) OR (relatedness_tier = 'Supplemental' AND (best_index < 11 OR best_index > 20))
rules.description: relatedness_tier must be consistent with best_index ranges: Primary-Short (1-5), Primary-Long (6-10), Supplemental (11-20).
rules.rationale: Logical model: relatedness_tier is derived deterministically from best_index. Any inconsistency between tier label and index value indicates a derivation bug.
